body_txt: At a party or other place where a lot of drinking is going on, especially if the place is far enough away to require driving there and back, there is usually one person, pre-selected or out of their own volition, who has to not drink at all in order to remain sober enough to drive the other partygoers/drinkers home safely. This person is known as the designated driver. This is Truth in Television , as, in most jurisdictions, in order to follow road safety laws, people aren't allowed to drive while drunk. However, in recent years, this practice has been somewhat in decline, especially with the rise of rideshare apps that make it more convenient to acquire rides. It should not be confused with other "designated X" tropes like Designated Hero or Designated Villain , which are YMMV (Designated Driver is not) and deal with something else entirely. If the designated driver's companions are doing things rather more dangerous than drinking, that's a Getaway Driver (although overlap is not impossible, depending on how able-bodied the drunk are ). A form of Harm Reduction . The Teetotaler makes a perfect candidate for the job, since they never drink anyway. Contrast the Drunk Driver , which we're trying to prevent here. Often crosses over with The Chaperone , as there's a chance they'll also have to make sure their drunk friends don't get too wild.
